senses.definition: the earth's atmosphere and the space beyond it

examples.x: She studied aerospace engineering to pursue a career in space exploration.
The aerospace industry plays a crucial role in developing advanced technologies for aviation and space travel.
The company specializes in aerospace manufacturing, producing components for aircraft and spacecraft.
The conference focused on the future of aerospace innovation and sustainability.
Advances in aerospace technology have led to more efficient and safer air travel around the world.

senses.definition: a branch of biology that deals with the study of life in space

examples.x: Astrobiology explores the potential for life beyond Earth, studying environments where life could exist.
Scientists engaged in astrobiology research investigate the conditions necessary for life to thrive on other planets.
The discovery of extremophiles has expanded the scope of astrobiology, revealing life forms that can survive in extreme environments.
Astrobiology involves interdisciplinary collaboration among biologists, chemists, astronomers, and geologists.
The search for biosignatures is a key focus of astrobiology, aiming to identify signs of life on distant planets and moons.

senses.definition: having a connection to or originating in the Earth's atmosphere

examples.x: Scientists study atmospheric chemistry to understand the composition of gases in the Earth's atmosphere.
The satellite collects data on atmospheric temperature changes to monitor global warming.
Atmospheric pollution from factories and vehicles contributes to air quality issues in urban areas.

senses.definition: related to the universe and the vast space outside the earth

examples.x: Cosmic phenomena encompass the vast and often mysterious aspects of the universe beyond Earth.
Cosmic expansion describes the ongoing increase in the scale of the universe.
Cosmic consciousness is a philosophical concept exploring humanity's connection to the universe.

senses.definition: the complete and round-shaped moon as seen from the earth

examples.x: The couple decided to take a stroll in the park under the full moon, enjoying a romantic evening.
Photographers gathered to capture the beauty of the landscape under the glow of the full moon.
The full moon illuminated the night sky, casting a soft glow over the landscape.
The tradition of werewolves transforming under a full moon has been popularized in folklore.
People gathered on the beach to watch the full moon rise above the horizon.

senses.definition: the moon when only half of its bright surface can be seen from the earth

examples.x: During the evening sky, a captivating half-moon hung just above the horizon.
Astronomers pointed their telescopes at the half-moon to study its cratered surface.
The garden party was illuminated by the soft glow of a half-moon overhead.
During the camping trip, they marveled at the bright half-moon lighting up the night sky.
The nighttime joggers enjoyed their run under the gentle glow of the half-moon.

senses.definition: the moon when only a small portion of its bright side is visible from the earth

examples.x: Skywatchers eagerly awaited the appearance of the new moon.
Many religious observances were tied to the appearance of the new moon.
During a new moon, the sky appears darker because the moon is not visible from Earth.
The planting of crops traditionally occurs during the new moon phase for optimal growth.
Astronomers study the alignment of celestial bodies during a new moon to observe faint stars and galaxies.

senses.definition: (of the sun or moon) to go out of sight below the horizon

examples.x: As we hiked to the hill's summit, we were treated to a spectacular view of the sun going down.

senses.definition: the explosion that, according to most scientists, caused the existence of the universe
the universe, particularly when it is thought of as a systematic whole

examples.x: The study of the cosmos encompasses the exploration of galaxies, stars, and planets.
Ancient civilizations developed myths and legends to explain the mysteries of the cosmos.
Astronomers use telescopes to observe distant phenomena in the cosmos.
Theoretical physicists investigate the fundamental laws governing the cosmos.
Understanding the cosmos requires interdisciplinary collaboration across astronomy, cosmology, and physics.

senses.definition: any natural object in the space, such as the sun, moon, etc.

examples.x: The moon is a celestial body that orbits the Earth.
Astronomers study celestial bodies such as stars, planets, and comets.
Jupiter is the largest celestial body in our solar system.
The International Space Station orbits Earth as a man-made celestial body.
Telescopes allow astronomers to observe distant celestial bodies in detail.

senses.definition: a pale band of light seen in the sky at night that contains the solar system and billions of other stars

examples.x: The Milky Way is a barred spiral galaxy that contains our solar system.
On a clear night, you can see the Milky Way stretching across the sky.
Astronomers study the structure and evolution of the Milky Way galaxy.
The Milky Way is home to billions of stars, including our sun.

senses.definition: a specific group of stars that form a pattern and have a name related to their shape

examples.x: Orion is a well-known constellation visible in the winter sky.
Ancient civilizations used constellations to navigate across the seas.
The Big Dipper is a prominent constellation consisting of seven bright stars.
Astronomers use telescopes to study distant galaxies located near constellations.
The constellation Cassiopeia forms a distinct "W" shape in the northern sky.

senses.definition: (astronomy) the celestial zone in the sky where the sun, moon, and planets appear to move, traditionally divided into twelve equal segments, each associated with a distinct name and symbol

examples.x: All twelve zodiac constellations can be seen within the zodiac belt.
Careful observation of the position of stars inside the zodiac was key for early astronomers to develop astrological ideas.
The zodiac is divided into twelve astrological signs, each representing different personality traits.
The constellation of Aries is associated with the first sign of the zodiac.
People born under the sign of Leo are said to possess strong leadership qualities, according to the zodiac.

senses.definition: an object in space that is a mass of ice and dust and when it nears the sun it starts illuminating in the shape of a tail

examples.x: Halley's Comet is one of the most famous comets, visible from Earth approximately every 76 years.
Comets are celestial objects composed of ice, dust, and rocky material.
The tail of a comet forms when solar radiation causes ice and dust to vaporize and stream away from the nucleus.
Astronomers study comets to learn about the early solar system and its composition.
The appearance of a bright comet in the night sky often attracts attention from amateur astronomers and stargazers alike.

senses.definition: a star that is relatively small in size or mass and is not very bright

examples.x: Brown dwarfs are substellar objects that are too large to be considered planets but too small to sustain hydrogen fusion.
The discovery of a new dwarf galaxy orbiting the Milky Way provided insights into galactic evolution.
Dwarf stars are often studied to understand stellar evolution and the life cycles of stars in the universe.

senses.definition: a planet that is outside the solar system

examples.x: Astronomers have discovered thousands of exoplanets orbiting stars beyond our solar system.
The search for habitable exoplanets is a key focus in the quest to find extraterrestrial life.
The Kepler Space Telescope has been instrumental in identifying distant exoplanets using the transit method.
Proxima b is an exoplanet orbiting Proxima Centauri, the closest star to the Sun.
Scientists use advanced telescopes and observatories to detect the faint signals of exoplanets orbiting distant stars.

senses.definition: a piece of rock coming from outer space that passes through the Earth's atmosphere, producing light

examples.x: We saw a bright meteor streak across the sky during the meteor shower last night.
Meteors, also known as shooting stars, are often visible as bright streaks of light in the night sky.
The study of meteors helps astronomers understand the composition of objects from the early solar system.
A meteorite is a meteor that survives its passage through the atmosphere and lands on Earth's surface.

senses.definition: a piece of rock or metal from space that has hit the surface of the earth

examples.x: Scientists analyzed the composition of the meteorite that fell in the desert last month.
Meteorites provide valuable information about the early solar system's formation.
The impact crater was formed by a large meteorite that struck Earth millions of years ago.
Collectors travel the world in search of rare and valuable meteorites for their collections.
The study of meteorites helps researchers understand the potential hazards of asteroids and comets.

senses.definition: a glowing cloud of gas and dust in outer space, often the result of a star explosion or formation
an exploding star that as a result is emitting a very large amount of light, more than the sun

examples.x: A supernova is a powerful explosion that occurs at the end of a massive star's life cycle.
The remnants of a supernova can form nebulae and enrich the interstellar medium with heavy elements.
Astronomers observed a distant galaxy where a supernova had recently occurred.

senses.definition: a US government agency responsible for space travel and the study of space

examples.x: NASA, the National Aeronautics and Space Administration, is responsible for the United States' civilian space program.
NASA's Mars rover successfully landed on the Martian surface, marking a significant achievement in planetary exploration.
Astronauts from NASA conducted experiments aboard the International Space Station to study microgravity effects on the human body.
NASA's Hubble Space Telescope has provided stunning images of distant galaxies and nebulae since its launch.
NASA's Artemis program aims to return astronauts to the Moon and establish a sustainable lunar presence by the 2020s.

senses.definition: an operation carried out in space

examples.x: The Apollo 11 mission successfully landed astronauts on the Moon in 1969.
The Mars Curiosity rover's mission is to explore the Martian surface and study its geology.
The Hubble Space Telescope has completed numerous scientific missions to observe distant galaxies and nebulae.
SpaceX's Crew Dragon completed its first manned mission to the International Space Station.
NASA's Voyager spacecraft embarked on a historic mission to explore the outer planets of our solar system.

senses.definition: an astronaut from Russia or the former Soviet Union

examples.x: Yuri Gagarin was the first cosmonaut to orbit the Earth in 1961.
Cosmonauts aboard the International Space Station conduct scientific experiments in microgravity.
Valentina Tereshkova was the first woman cosmonaut to travel to space in 1963.
Russian cosmonauts train extensively at the Yuri Gagarin Cosmonaut Training Center before space missions.
The Soviet Union launched several successful cosmonaut missions during the Space Race with the United States.

senses.definition: (of a spacecraft or aircraft) to leave the ground, particularly vertically

examples.x: The rocket lifted off with a thunderous roar, marking the beginning of the historic mission.
The tension was palpable as the countdown reached zero and the aircraft lifted off, soaring into the sky.
The airplane taxied down the runway, its engines gaining power, and then lifted off smoothly into the clear blue sky.
The spectators watched in awe as the helicopter lifted off, its rotors spinning rapidly, ready to transport the injured hiker to safety.
The small experimental aircraft lifted off smoothly, its pilot eager to test its capabilities.

senses.definition: an imaginary line in the middle of an object around which the object revolves

examples.x: The spinning top rotates around its axis, demonstrating the principles of angular momentum.
The Earth's North and South Poles are located at opposite ends of its rotational axis.
The axis of a gyroscope allows it to maintain stability and orientation during motion.
The rotation of planets around their axes determines their day and night cycles.

senses.definition: the action of circular movement around a fixed point

examples.x: The rotation of the Earth influences the direction of ocean currents and weather patterns.
The rotation of a wind turbine's blades generates electricity from wind energy.
Planets in our solar system exhibit varying rates of rotation, affecting their day lengths.
The rotation of tires on a vehicle ensures even wear and extends their lifespan.

senses.definition: a vehicle designed and used to go to space and return multiple times
a mysterious object that some people claim to have seen flying around in the sky and assume that it is a spaceship from another world

examples.x: Witnesses reported seeing an unidentified flying object hovering silently in the night sky.
The military investigated multiple reports of unidentified flying objects near the military base.
Scientists study reports of unidentified flying objects to determine if they have natural or man-made origins.
Many people believe that unidentified flying objects could be evidence of extraterrestrial life visiting Earth.
The pilots reported encountering an unidentified flying object that moved at high speeds and changed direction abruptly.

senses.definition: having or seeming to have no or little weight, caused by the absence of gravity

examples.x: Objects inside the spacecraft become weightless once it reaches orbit around the Earth.
The sensation of weightless flight during parabolic maneuvers simulates the feeling of space travel.
During the dive of the aircraft, passengers felt briefly weightless before gravity reasserted itself.
During a zero-gravity flight, passengers enjoy the sensation of being weightless for short periods.
